title: 2-Amino-7-phosphonoheptanoic acid as an amino acid antagonist
journal: Canadian Journal of Physiology and Pharmacology
abstract: The optical isomers of 2-amino-7-phosphoheptanoate (APH), the phosphonic acid analogue of α-aminosuberic acid, have been examined for their ability to block the excitation of rat spinal neurones induced by various acidic amino acids. Both isomers were active, although the D(−) form was approximately twice as potent. The pattern of action of APH indicated that it reacts with N-methyl-D-aspartate-preferring receptors.
